fingerprint: H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc adopted wholesale from globe cosmology; λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc IS the Hubble length; ρ_A formula is MOND relabeled; galaxy distances from globe-calibrated ladders

globe_values.globe_equivalent: c/H₀ = Hubble length (standard cosmological distance scale)
globe_values.match_precision: exact (by definition — dome defines λ_A = c/H₀)
globe_values.derivation_from_dome: none — H₀ has no path from disc_radius=20,015 km or firmament_height=9,086 km

globe_values.globe_equivalent: Hubble constant measured from Type Ia supernovae distance ladder (SH0ES: 73.04±1.04) and CMB power spectrum (Planck: 67.4±0.5), both requiring ΛCDM + GR framework
globe_values.derivation_from_dome: none — dome acknowledges it takes H₀ as input ('ECM uses H₀=70 middle value')

globe_values.globe_equivalent: MOND interpolation function: g_obs = g_bar × ν(g_bar/a₀). Mapping: ρ_A↔g_obs, ρ_bar↔g_bar, g†↔a₀. Milgrom (1983), derived from galaxy rotation curves in standard galactic coordinate framework
globe_values.derivation_from_dome: none — formula appears undeveloped on model.html with no definition of symbols, no connection to dome geometry, no derivation of g† from dome parameters

globe_values.dome_label: Galaxy distances (Virgo, Fornax, NGC 5584, Coma, Perseus)
globe_values.globe_equivalent: Measured via Cepheid period-luminosity relation and Surface Brightness Fluctuation method, calibrated to standard cosmological distance ladder
globe_values.match_precision: exact — dome uses published globe-framework distances as inputs
globe_values.derivation_from_dome: none — dome has no distance measurement framework (firmament at 9,086 km, galaxies at millions of light-years)

why_it_matters: WIN-052 is entirely constructed from globe-derived inputs. The Hubble constant H₀ can only be measured from galaxy redshift-distance relations interpreted through Friedmann cosmology (GR on cosmological scales). The dome takes H₀, computes c/H₀, relabels it as 'aetheric wavelength,' then claims galaxy redshift-distance consistency — but this is circular, since H₀ was measured using the same galaxy distances the dome now 'confirms.' The RAR formula ρ_A = g₀ρ_bar/g† is MOND's interpolation function relabeled with aetheric vocabulary. The galaxy distances themselves are measured using globe-calibrated distance ladders. Every input to WIN-052 originates from globe-based astrophysics; the dome contributes zero original content.

found_in.wins.html: WIN-052 exists as static HTML claim only. Claims z = D/λ_A where λ_A = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc. Cites 5 galaxies/clusters. H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc stated as 'ECM baseline.'
found_in.model.html: Single undeveloped formula: 'RAR: ρ_A = g₀×ρ_bar / g†' under Tesla Principle #3. ρ_A = 3.4×10³⁷ kg/m³ stated. No derivation, no symbol definitions, no connection to dome geometry.
found_in.formulas.json: FORM-050 (g_aetheric) docstring: 'Aetheric pressure model: same formula as WGS84' — dome's own code admits gravity model uses globe geometry (Somigliana/WGS84 formula).
found_in.inject_ai_layer.py: Only WIN-001 through WIN-026. No RAR, no galaxy content.
found_in.predictions.js: Only WIN-001 through WIN-026. No RAR.
found_in.api/current/predictions.json: Only WIN-001 through WIN-032. No RAR.
found_in.api/current/parameters.json: No H₀, no λ_A, no g†, no cosmological parameters whatsoever.

code_search.notes: WIN-052 has ZERO code implementation across the entire repository. No script computes RAR predictions, no monitoring domain tracks galaxy dynamics, no formula derives the RAR from dome parameters. The formula on model.html (ρ_A = g₀ρ_bar/g†) is a placeholder with no code backing. The cosmological parameters (H₀, λ_A, galaxy distances) are not even defined in parameters.json — they exist only as text on the static HTML wins page. FORM-050's docstring ('same formula as WGS84') is a bonus finding: the dome's own codebase admits its gravity model replicates globe physics.

additional_findings.FORM-050_admission: FORM-050 (g_aetheric) in formulas.json has raw_body containing: 'Aetheric pressure model: same formula as WGS84'. The formula g_eq * (1 + alpha * sin²(lat)) is the International Gravity Formula / Somigliana equation, which models gravity on an oblate spheroid. This is the dome's own code acknowledging it uses globe-derived gravity. While not directly about WIN-052's galaxy-scale RAR, it demonstrates the systematic pattern: dome labels globe physics as 'aetheric' without changing the math.
additional_findings.z_equals_hubble_law: WIN-052 references z = D/λ_A where λ_A = c/H₀. This is algebraically identical to Hubble's Law: z = v/c = H₀D/c = D/(c/H₀) = D/λ_A. The dome has taken Hubble's Law, renamed c/H₀ as 'aetheric wavelength,' and presented the same equation as an ECM prediction. The agreement with galaxy observations is by construction — it IS the same equation that was calibrated to those observations.
additional_findings.ontology_gap: The dome's parameters.json contains disc_radius=20,015 km and firmament_height=9,086 km. WIN-052 references galaxies at 16.5–100 Mpc (5×10¹⁹ to 3×10²¹ km). Ratio: ~10¹⁵ to 10¹⁷. The dome provides no ontology for extragalactic objects — where do galaxies exist in a geometry capped at ~9,086 km?

suggested_text: The dome's RAR claim borrows every input from globe-based astrophysics. The Hubble constant H₀ = 70 km/s/Mpc — used to define the dome's λ<sub>A</sub> = c/H₀ = 4,283 Mpc — can only be measured from galaxy redshift-distance relations interpreted through Friedmann cosmology. The dome admits H₀ is an adopted baseline, not derived from dome geometry. The RAR formula ρ<sub>A</sub> = g₀ρ<sub>bar</sub>/g† is MOND's interpolation function (Milgrom 1983) relabeled: ρ<sub>A</sub>↔g<sub>obs</sub>, ρ<sub>bar</sub>↔g<sub>bar</sub>, g†↔a₀. No script in the dome repository computes RAR predictions, no monitoring domain tracks galaxy dynamics, and the cosmological parameters don't even appear in the dome's own API parameter file.
